cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: Estimating the Replicability of Sport Science Research
cluster_decisions.reason: The OSF node metadata explicitly describes an analysis repository containing effect sizes from original and replication studies plus analysis code and markdown summaries. The sports-science corpus is already integrated locally from public supplemental outcome/sample-size artifacts, so this node is a child/source-family artifact to inventory rather than a new root row.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Attach OSF node sfbva and related project OSF 3VUFG as source-family access routes under corpus_db_sports_science_supplemental_outcomes_table; mirror or verify file-level effect-size/code artifacts if not already represented by the local sports supplement table and sample-size workbook.

lead_level_notes.notes: OSF sfbva project metadata for a source family already integrated via the sports supplemental outcomes table.

cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: internal current peer-reviewed main-result status table
cluster_decisions.reason: This is an internal derived status table summarizing whether candidate corpora have peer-reviewed D/N and main-result flags. It is useful routing context, but it is not an external source family, dataset, repository, or result table for Figure 1 extraction.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Keep as pipeline context only; do not add to source-family intake and do not route as a result table.

lead_level_notes.notes: Internal derived status row table; not an external source object.
Duplicate hit for the same internal derived status table.

cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: Replication of null results secondary analysis
cluster_decisions.reason: This eLife article is a secondary methods/analysis paper illustrating equivalence testing and Bayes factors using already-known data from RP:CB, EPRP, and RPP. Its linked code repository may be useful provenance context, but the cluster is not a new reusable original-replication source family for Figure 1.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Keep as context for null-result interpretation and possible transformation code; use canonical RP:CB, EPRP, and RPP source-family artifacts for extraction rather than promoting this article as a new corpus.

lead_level_notes.notes: Secondary analysis article over existing source families; retain as context.
Versioned DOI hit for the same eLife article.

cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: Life Outcomes of Personality Replication Project (LOOPR)
cluster_decisions.reason: LOOPR is already an integrated live source family with a local coding workbook containing original and replication effects for personality-outcome associations. The DOI/OSF hits in this cluster are source-family aliases and bibliographic/version artifacts for that existing parent.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Inventory DOI, OSF, and publication-version aliases under corpus_db_loopr_coding_workbook; keep the local Replication coding and results.xlsx workbook as the result-bearing artifact for extraction/provenance work.

lead_level_notes.notes: Bibliographic/root lead for LOOPR; merge under the existing integrated LOOPR parent.
OSF/source-family hit; inventory as access/provenance under LOOPR.

cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: Reproducibility Project: Psychology canonical OSF data
cluster_decisions.reason: The cluster contains local RPP CSV mirrors. The canonical OSF CSV is already integrated live, and the jtLeek file is an archival mirror/cross-check. These are result-table artifacts under RPP, not a new source family.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Route rpp_data_osf.csv and rpp_data_jtleek.csv to the result-table worklist for provenance extraction and mirror/cross-check mapping under corpus_db_rpp_canonical_osf_csv.

lead_level_notes.notes: Canonical RPP OSF CSV mirror; primary result-table artifact.
jtLeek archival mirror of RPP data; cross-check artifact under RPP.

cluster_decisions.reason: The cluster is a dataset-level package from the curated awesome-replicability-data repository. It contains original and replication cleaned CSVs plus processing code and download manifests. These are child artifacts under the GitHub source family, not separate root source families.

cluster_decisions.reason: The local combined_data.csv table enumerates original, replication, additional, and rescue rows with N, raw statistical text, original article links, preregistration links, and replication-score fields. This is a reusable result table, not a new source-family root.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Route combined_data.csv, combined_data.md, and parsed_data.csv to result-table extraction under raw_replication_project:rescue_251; preserve raw_stat and N text before any D/N conversion or row collapsing.

parsed_data.csv is supporting parsed table output.

cluster_decisions.reason: The cluster contains local code/data files from a PSA006 trolley multi-lab package. It appears to be a reusable project package, but the compact evidence is mostly analysis/helper code, so the package needs inventory before any result-table extraction or corpus promotion.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Inventory PSA006 trolley files under lead_harvest:psa_006_trolley; inspect aggregate_inferences, additional-table, plotting scripts, and data outputs for original/replication effect rows and sample sizes before routing specific tables to extraction.

lead_level_notes.notes: aggregate_inferences.R is analysis code; inspect outputs before result-table routing.
calculate_additional_table.R may generate a candidate table; keep as code context until outputs are confirmed.
cultural_distance.R is covariate/data-prep code, not a result table by itself.

cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: Successes and Failures of Replications based on OSF Registries
cluster_decisions.reason: The article/preprint describes a meta-analysis of independent replication studies registered on OSF and points to open data/code, including an OSF project and dataset. It may be a reusable source object, but its rows need inventory because only a subset appear to contain comparable original and replication effect sizes.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Keep as a source-family candidate and inventory OSF project 9r62x plus dataset sfdq4/code scripts; route only confirmed original/replication effect-size rows to result-table extraction.

lead_level_notes.notes: Preprint/source-family lead; keep for OSF data/code inventory.
Duplicate DOI/preprint hit for the same source-family candidate.

cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: SCORE power and sample-size methods package
cluster_decisions.reason: The local Tyner/Nosek OSF package is a SCORE-related methods/materials package containing sample-size templates, power scripts, manuals, and an analysis/code archive. These are useful source-family artifacts under SCORE, but the compact nodes are not themselves result tables.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Inventory README, OSF file list, power/sample-size templates, and analysis data/code archive under SCORE; route only any confirmed result-bearing data tables from the archive to extraction.

cluster_decisions.preferred_source_family_name: Triplett social facilitation Registered Replication Report
cluster_decisions.reason: This is an individual registered replication report of Triplett's social facilitation experiment, with OSF/source links. It has affirmative replication-relation evidence, but the cluster does not establish a reusable multi-row source-family table.
cluster_decisions.next_action: Route the DOI/OSF package to the individual replication-paper worklist; inspect OSF pages for value-bearing files only if extracting this one paper/package separately.
